title: Uniola thalassica
intro: Uniola thalassica, also known as sea grass or beach grass, is a type of coastal grass that grows in the salt marshes and beaches of the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Uniola thalassica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Uniola thalassica include teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various skin conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Uniola thalassica is a species of grass. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This classification is based on the botanical characteristics of the plant, including its leaf structure and flowering patterns. Uniola thalassica is further classified within the genus Uniola, which consists of several species of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Uniola thalassica is a part of the broader system of plant taxonomy.
